Real Before / After

Your photo is your
storefront window.

On a delivery app, you have 1.2 seconds to stop the scroll. A bad photo means the customer moves on — to one of the 30,000 other options available. A great photo creates an instant craving.

A physical restaurant menu is read at a table, with a waiter, in a context where the customer is already committed to eating there. A delivery app menu is different — it's competitive, visual, and instant.

The photo doesn't just have to look good. It has to sell. AI-generated or not, every image on your profile needs to be laser-focused on making the customer click.

Before — Standard photo

Ranking
After DeliveryEdge
5.2%
Click Rate
$27
Avg Order
#3
Ranking

Same burger. Same recipe. Same price. The only difference is the photo direction, the lighting, and the visual strategy. The result: +189% clicks, +50% average order, ranking jumped from #52 to #3 on the platform. This is not a coincidence — it's optimization.

Case Study — Tito's Kitchen

Same product. Same kitchen.
Completely different results.

Tito's Kitchen launched in Canada in 2022. The product was excellent from day one. The platform presence wasn't. Here's the full before and after.

Before — Generic restaurant shot

Generic plate photo. No branding. Not designed for delivery apps. The customer doesn't know what they're ordering or why they should click.

2.1%
Click Rate
$16
Avg Order
#68
Ranking
After — Tito's Kitchen

Branded packaging. Studio photography. The customer instantly knows the product, the brand, and wants it.

6.4%
Click Rate
$26
Avg Order
#1
Ranking
Before — Unbranded menu

Cheap foil trays. No identity. No combo logic. Customer sees a basic meal with no story.

After — Optimized combo

Branded black tray. Meal deal structure visible. Clean top-down shot. Average order went from $16 to $26.

A ghost kitchen has no storefront, no foot traffic, no word of mouth. Every customer starts with a photo, a name, and a price. On Uber Eats, you're one of hundreds of options. The photo, the packaging and the menu structure are the only things that make someone click you over the restaurant right next to you.

What We Optimize

Five things.
Everything changes.

We don't do everything. We do five specific things — and we do them better than anyone because we've lived them on our own restaurants for 6+ years.

01
🏗️
Menu Engineering

Category structure, item ordering, hero product placement, naming, descriptions. Your menu needs to guide the customer — not overwhelm them.

02
📸
Photo Strategy

AI-assisted or real photography — it doesn't matter. What matters is that each photo creates an immediate craving and communicates exactly what the customer will receive.

03
💰
Pricing & Promotions

The right promo at the right time can double your volume. The wrong one destroys your margin. We build a promotion strategy that grows revenue — not just orders.

04
📊
Platform Optimization

Uber Eats and DoorDash have algorithms. We know them — because we live under them every day on our own restaurants. We optimize your profile to rank higher and convert better.

05
🚀
Concept Launch

Launching a new ghost kitchen or delivery brand from scratch? We build the full foundation before your first order comes in — menu, visuals, platform setup, all done.

💬
A word of honesty

We help restaurants that already have a good product but are invisible on the platforms. We optimize what exists — we don't create demand out of nothing. If your food is good and your platform presence is weak, we can make a real difference. If the food itself is the problem, no amount of optimization will fix it. We're not $10,000 consultants — we're operators who know what works, at a price that makes sense.

Our Story

Not a consultant.
An operator.

DeliveryEdge was born from 6+ years of operating restaurants and ghost kitchens in Miami. We went through the ghost kitchen boom of 2020, learned everything the hard way, and built systems that actually work.

We've seen what happens when a restaurant has great food but terrible platform execution. We've also seen what happens when you fix it. That's exactly what we do for you.

2019
Canada · Toronto

The beginning

Started in Canada, studying delivery platforms from the inside. While most operators were guessing, we were already understanding how the algorithm worked and what really drove orders.

2020
Ghost Kitchen

Brick'n'Cheese launches

Launched Brick'n'Cheese in Canada. First ghost kitchen built from zero — menu, photos, pricing, platform strategy. Tested everything. Kept what worked.

🏆 #1 ranked ghost kitchen on the platform
2022
Canada · Ghost Kitchen

Tito's Kitchen launches

Second ghost kitchen concept launched in Canada. Applied everything learned from Brick'n'Cheese. Faster results, stronger positioning from day one.

🇺🇸 Expansion to the United States begins
2025
United States & Canada · Worldwide

4 concepts. 4 cities. DeliveryEdge.

Operating 4 restaurant concepts across cities in the US and Canada. The systems we built work on any market, any platform. Now we're making them available to other operators.

FAQ

Questions we get
all the time.

My food is good — why do I need optimization?
Good food is the minimum. On a delivery app, a customer doesn't taste your food before ordering — they see a photo, a name, and a price. If those three things aren't optimized, the best kitchen in the city stays invisible. We help the right people find you and choose you over the hundreds of other options on the screen.
Can you guarantee results?
We don't guarantee specific numbers — no one honest does. What we do guarantee is a complete, professional optimization based on 6+ years of real delivery operations. The restaurants we've worked on, including our own, consistently see improvements in click rate, average order value, and platform ranking. But we're honest: if your food or operations have fundamental issues, optimization alone won't fix them.
How long does it take?
Tier 1 (strategy only) is typically delivered within 5–7 days after the strategy call. Tier 2 (with visuals) takes 7–10 days. Tier 3 (full package) takes 10–14 days. If you need it faster, we can often accommodate — just mention it on the call.
Do you work with any type of restaurant?
We work with any restaurant or ghost kitchen that is on — or planning to launch on — Uber Eats or DoorDash in the US or Canada. We're most effective with operators who have a solid product but struggle with their platform presence. We don't work with restaurants that have fundamental operational issues — we're not a restaurant rescue service, we're a delivery performance specialist.
What's included in the free strategy call?
30 minutes. No pitch, no hard sell. We review your current profile together, identify your biggest opportunities, and give you 2–3 specific things you can do this week — whether you hire us or not. If there's a fit, we'll discuss which package makes sense for your situation. If there isn't, you still leave with actionable insights.
Are the AI-generated food photos convincing?
When done right, yes — and the results speak for themselves. The goal isn't photorealism, it's appetite appeal. A well-crafted AI visual with the right lighting, composition and styling consistently outperforms a mediocre real photo. We've tested both extensively. That said, if you already have quality real photos, we'll use them. AI is a tool, not a default.
What's the payment structure?
50% deposit to start, 50% on delivery. All payments via Stripe — secure, fast, and you get an invoice automatically. No contracts, no retainers, no hidden fees. The price you see is the price you pay.
Will this replace my marketing efforts?
No — and it's important to be clear about that. DeliveryEdge optimizes your presence on delivery apps. That's the foundation. Your social media, your community, your word of mouth: all of that amplifies what we build together. An optimized profile without traffic converts better. Traffic without an optimized profile gets wasted. You need both. We handle one side of it — the one most restaurants neglect.
Is this a one-time fix or ongoing work?
One engagement. We deliver a fully optimized profile, a clear strategy, and ready-to-use visuals. After that, you manage it — or come back when you launch a new concept. We're not a monthly subscription. We give you the tools and the system, then you run it. Most clients don't need to come back for 6 to 12 months, if ever.
What if my results don't improve after the service?
Results depend on many factors: your product, your area, your competition, and how well you implement the recommendations. We optimize everything visible on the platform — not your kitchen, not your operations, not your logistics. If you apply what we deliver, you have every reason to see improvement. But we can't act for you, and we're honest about that from day one. That's exactly why the strategy call exists — to make sure there's a real opportunity before we work together.
Do I still need to be active on social media?
Yes. Delivery apps are one channel — not the whole picture. A customer who finds you on Instagram and then sees a clean, professional profile on Uber Eats is much more likely to order. An optimized profile with no external visibility still works, but slower. We give you the best possible profile — you bring people to it. Think of us as setting up the best storefront possible. You still need to tell people where the store is.

Ready to stop leaving
money on the table?

Free 30-minute call. No pitch. Just an honest look at what's costing you orders right now.
